sections.subtitle: Recent Form
sections.text: Bayer 04 Leverkusen are in strong form with an unbeaten streak of 7 matches, often scoring first and seeing over 2.5 goals in most games. 1. FC Union Berlin have had mixed results with 2 wins, 1 draw, and 2 losses in their last 5 matches, showing inconsistency but capability to score.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: Leverkusen have dominated Union Berlin historically, with no losses in the last 9 encounters and winning 3 of the last 5 matchups. The most recent game ended in a 0-0 draw, indicating Union Berlin's potential to hold firm defensively.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Leverkusen rank higher in creating big chances (3rd in league) and have strong ball possession (5th), while Union Berlin struggle defensively, conceding 11 goals this season. Leverkusen's attacking metrics and duel-winning ability (1st in aerial duels) give them an edge.

sections.subtitle: Key Factors
sections.text: Leverkusen’s consistent scoring and Union Berlin’s recent defensive lapses suggest Leverkusen will likely control the game. However, Union Berlin’s recent high-scoring game (4-3 win) shows they can be dangerous on the counter.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Half-time overview
halftimeSections.text: At half-time, Bayer 04 Leverkusen lead 1-0 against 1. FC Union Berlin at the BayArena. The home side has dominated possession and created more structured attacking opportunities, with Ernest Poku's goal in the 33rd minute proving decisive so far. Union Berlin, despite being on the back foot for much of the half, have shown flashes of danger, particularly with a couple of big chances created but ultimately squandered. The contrast in ball possession and final third entries highlights Leverkusen's control, while Union will be frustrated with their inability to convert promising moments into goals.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Leverkusen's attacking dominance
halftimeSections.text: Bayer 04 Leverkusen have been the more assertive side in attack, showing consistent intent to push forward and create openings. Their dominance in the final third is evident through numerous entries and successful penetrations into the penalty area. The home team capitalized on their pressure with a well-taken goal, showcasing their ability to turn sustained play into results. Key players like Lucas Vázquez and Ernest Poku have been instrumental in driving the attack, with contributions in creating and finishing chances.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Union Berlin's missed opportunities
halftimeSections.text: Despite being under significant pressure, 1. FC Union Berlin managed to carve out a couple of notable chances in the first half. Their attacking forays, though less frequent, showed potential, particularly with entries into the penalty area and corners earned. However, their inability to convert big chances into goals has left them trailing. Players like Woo-Yeong Jeong and Andrej Ilić have been involved in threatening positions, but poor finishing has cost them dearly at this stage.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum shifts
halftimeSections.text: The first half saw Bayer 04 Leverkusen maintaining the upper hand in attack momentum for the majority of the time, with consistent spells of pressure that kept Union Berlin on the defensive. There were brief moments where Union managed to shift the momentum in their favor, particularly around the middle and towards the end of the half, pushing forward with intent. However, Leverkusen quickly regained control with intense attacking spells, especially leading up to and following their goal, underlining their ability to dictate the tempo of the game.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Defensive and tactical observations
halftimeSections.text: Defensively, Leverkusen have been solid, with players like Edmond Tapsoba and Jarell Quansah contributing to key clearances and maintaining a strong backline. Their high percentage of aerial duels won has helped them neutralize Union's attempts to play long balls. On the other hand, Union Berlin's defense has been under constant pressure, with players like Diogo Leite and Danilho Doekhi making important interventions but struggling to cope with Leverkusen's relentless final third play. Tactically, Leverkusen’s approach of dominating possession has worked well, while Union may need to adjust to exploit counter-attacking opportunities more effectively.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Second half outlook
halftimeSections.text: Looking ahead to the second half, Bayer 04 Leverkusen are likely to continue their high-possession style, aiming to extend their lead with further attacking pressure. Ernest Poku, already on the scoresheet, could be a key figure in adding to their tally if given space. Union Berlin, trailing by a single goal, will need to take more risks to equalize, potentially leaving gaps at the back. If they can capitalize on their missed chances from the first half, a comeback isn’t out of the question, but they’ll need to be clinical. The match could see more goals, given both teams’ ability to create chances, though Leverkusen remain favorites to secure the win.
